__fixture__.name: update_candidate_version_mismatch
__fixture__.scenario: Update screen launch preserving candidate identity, running-build truth, and private support routing when the offered update and local runtime posture disagree.

title: Update candidate reports incompatible local runtime

source_object.object_summary: The update screen is the source object, not just a generic app state, because the mismatch only appears on one concrete update offer.

current_state_rows.summary: The offered update is visible but cannot be applied from the current runtime posture.
The update screen shows a concrete incompatibility rather than an unknown or stale state.
A known-limit row is already attached to the mismatch, so the packet preserves that context.

build_context.build_summary: The packet captures both the running exact build and the offered update build so support can reason about the skew mechanically.

route_context.route_summary: The packet was launched from a local review of one update candidate row, not from a later install attempt.

scope_and_boundary_context.scope_summary: The issue is anchored to the update candidate object itself and does not depend on a workspace path.

provenance_bindings.note: Specific update offer row shown in the update screen.
Optional support bundle preserving installer and runtime inventory.

evidence_and_recovery_context.consent_summary: The packet includes the banner and support-bundle refs only; no installer logs are embedded directly.

destination_context.destination_summary: The issue is routed to the private support portal because the mismatch depends on machine-local runtime posture.

export_safe_summary: Preserves the update-offer id, current and target build refs, known-limit link, and private support route for the version-mismatch case.
user_selected_note: The update screen offers 1.0.3 but marks the local runtime as incompatible.
